title: Modeling batch effect in population-scale scRNA-seq data
text: scDesignPop can model population-scale scRNA-seq data batch covariates enables simulation datasets user-specified batch effects, including scenarios batch effects added removed.

title: Modeling batch effect in population-scale scRNA-seq data
text: , use example SingleCellExperiment object example_sce 982982 genes 79987998 cells example eQTL genotype dataframe example_eqtlgeno demonstrate main tutorial. two objects contains gene expression SNP genotypes 4040 anonymized individuals eQTL genotype dataframe provides 27922792 putative cell-type-specific eQTLs. example_sce object also includes pseudo-batch covariate, batch labels randomly assigned 40 individuals individual level applied cells belonging individual.
code: library(scDesignPop) library(SingleCellExperiment) library(SummarizedExperiment) library(ggplot2) theme_set(theme_bw())  data("example_sce") data("example_eqtlgeno") colData(example_sce) #> DataFrame with 7998 rows and 5 columns #>             indiv     pool cell_type      sex       age #>          <factor> <factor>  <factor> <factor> <numeric> #> Cell1      SAMP30        1    bmem          2        76 #> Cell2      SAMP30        1    monoc         2        76 #> Cell3      SAMP30        1    mononc        2        76 #> Cell4      SAMP30        1    monoc         2        76 #> Cell5      SAMP30        1    monoc         2        76 #> ...           ...      ...       ...      ...       ... #> Cell7994   SAMP35        2     cd4nc        2        73 #> Cell7995   SAMP35        2     cd4nc        2        73 #> Cell7996   SAMP35        2     cd4nc        2        73 #> Cell7997   SAMP35        2     cd4nc        2        73 #> Cell7998   SAMP35        2     cd4nc        2        73

title: Modeling batch effect in population-scale scRNA-seq data
text: run scDesignPop, list data required input. done using constructDataPop function. SingleCellExperiment object eqtlgeno dataframe two main inputs needed. eqtlgeno dataframe consists eQTL annotations (must cell state, gene, SNP, chromosome, position columns minimum), genotypes across individuals (columns) every SNP (rows). structure example eqtlgeno dataframe given .
code: data_list <- constructDataPop(     sce = example_sce,     eqtlgeno_df = example_eqtlgeno,     new_covariate = as.data.frame(colData(example_sce)),     copula_variable = "cell_type",     slot_name = "counts",     snp_mode = "single"     )

title: Modeling batch effect in population-scale scRNA-seq data
text: Next, marginal model specified fit gene using fitMarginalPop function. use Negative Binominal parametric model using "nb". Since want include batch effect modeling, specify mean_formula = "(1|indiv) + cell_type + pool. , use pseudo-batches show scDesignPop can model alter batch effects.
code: marginal_list <- fitMarginalPop(     data_list = data_list,     mean_formula = "(1|indiv) + cell_type + pool",     model_family = "nb",     interact_colnames = "cell_type",     parallelization = "parallel",     n_threads = 20L     )

title: Modeling batch effect in population-scale scRNA-seq data
text: third step fit Gaussian copula using fitCopulaPop function.
code: set.seed(123, kind = "L'Ecuyer-CMRG")  copula_fit <- fitCopulaPop(     sce = example_sce,     assay_use = "counts",     input_data = data_list[["covariate"]],     marginal_list = marginal_list,     family_use = "nb",     copula = "gaussian",     n_cores = 2L,     parallelization = "parallel"     )  RNGkind("Mersenne-Twister")  # reset

title: Modeling batch effect in population-scale scRNA-seq data
text: fourth step compute mean, sigma, zero probability parameters using extractParaPop function.
code: para_new <- extractParaPop(     sce = example_sce,     assay_use = "counts",     marginal_list = marginal_list,     n_cores = 2L,     family_use = "nb",     new_covariate = data_list[["new_covariate"]],     new_eqtl_geno_list = data_list[["eqtl_geno_list"]],     data = data_list[["covariate"]],     parallelization = "parallel"     )

title: Modeling batch effect in population-scale scRNA-seq data
text: fifth step simulate counts using simuNewPop function.
code: set.seed(123, kind = "L'Ecuyer-CMRG")  newcount_mat <- simuNewPop(     sce = example_sce,     mean_mat = para_new[["mean_mat"]],     sigma_mat = para_new[["sigma_mat"]],     zero_mat = para_new[["zero_mat"]],     copula_list = copula_fit[["copula_list"]],     n_cores = 2L,     family_use = "nb",     input_data = data_list[["covariate"]],     new_covariate = data_list[["new_covariate"]],     important_feature = copula_fit[["important_feature"]],     filtered_gene = data_list[["filtered_gene"]],     parallelization = "parallel"     )  RNGkind("Mersenne-Twister")  # reset

what: Step 6: create SingleCellExperiment object using simulated data
title: Modeling batch effect in population-scale scRNA-seq data
text: simulating data, can create SingleCellExperiment object follows.
code: simu_sce <- SingleCellExperiment(list(counts = newcount_mat),                                   colData = data_list[["new_covariate"]]) names(assays(simu_sce)) <- "counts"  # save gene meta data rowData(simu_sce) <- rowData(example_sce)

what: Step 1: set new batch effect sizes in marginal models
title: Modeling batch effect in population-scale scRNA-seq data
text: Check rank batch effect sizes marginal distributions. , take gene ENSG00000023902 example since every gene fitted using formula. ranks batch effects obtained, set new value gene marginal models check summary report .
code: marginal_list$ENSG00000023902$fit #> Formula:           #> response ~ (1 | indiv) + cell_type + pool + `1:150159616` + `1:150159616`:cell_type #> Data: res_list[["dmat_df"]] #>       AIC       BIC    logLik  df.resid  #>  8849.014  8925.870 -4413.507      7987  #> Random-effects (co)variances: #>  #> Conditional model: #>  Groups Name        Std.Dev. #>  indiv  (Intercept) 0.1589   #>  #> Number of obs: 7998 / Conditional model: indiv, 40 #>  #> Dispersion parameter for nbinom2 family (): 1.57  #>  #> Fixed Effects: #>  #> Conditional model: #>                   (Intercept)                 cell_typecd4nc   #>                      -1.30611                       -1.36419   #>                cell_typemonoc                cell_typemononc   #>                       0.41109                        0.44567   #>                         pool2                  `1:150159616`   #>                      -0.03313                       -0.06421   #>  cell_typecd4nc:`1:150159616`   cell_typemonoc:`1:150159616`   #>                       0.06084                        0.10866   #> cell_typemononc:`1:150159616`   #>                       0.27336 marginal_list_diff <- lapply(marginal_list, function(x) {   val <- rnorm(1, mean = 10, sd = 2) # set zero for batch effect removal   x$fit$fit$par[5] <- val   x$fit$fit$parfull[5] <- val   x$fit$sdr$par.fixed[5] <- val   x })  marginal_list_diff$ENSG00000023902$fit #> Formula:           #> response ~ (1 | indiv) + cell_type + pool + `1:150159616` + `1:150159616`:cell_type #> Data: res_list[["dmat_df"]] #>       AIC       BIC    logLik  df.resid  #>  8849.014  8925.870 -4413.507      7987  #> Random-effects (co)variances: #>  #> Conditional model: #>  Groups Name        Std.Dev. #>  indiv  (Intercept) 0.1589   #>  #> Number of obs: 7998 / Conditional model: indiv, 40 #>  #> Dispersion parameter for nbinom2 family (): 1.57  #>  #> Fixed Effects: #>  #> Conditional model: #>                   (Intercept)                 cell_typecd4nc   #>                      -1.30611                       -1.36419   #>                cell_typemonoc                cell_typemononc   #>                       0.41109                        0.44567   #>                         pool2                  `1:150159616`   #>                       9.93531                       -0.06421   #>  cell_typecd4nc:`1:150159616`   cell_typemonoc:`1:150159616`   #>                       0.06084                        0.10866   #> cell_typemononc:`1:150159616`   #>                       0.27336

title: Modeling batch effect in population-scale scRNA-seq data
code: para_new_diff <- extractParaPop(     sce = example_sce,     assay_use = "counts",     marginal_list = marginal_list_diff,     n_cores = 2L,     family_use = "nb",     new_covariate = data_list[["new_covariate"]],     new_eqtl_geno_list = data_list[["eqtl_geno_list"]],     data = data_list[["covariate"]],     parallelization = "parallel"     )

what: Step 3: simulate counts with batch effects
title: Modeling batch effect in population-scale scRNA-seq data
code: set.seed(123, kind = "L'Ecuyer-CMRG")  newcount_mat_diff <- simuNewPop(     sce = example_sce,     mean_mat = para_new_diff[["mean_mat"]],     sigma_mat = para_new_diff[["sigma_mat"]],     zero_mat = para_new_diff[["zero_mat"]],     copula_list = copula_fit[["copula_list"]],     n_cores = 2L,     family_use = "nb",     input_data = data_list[["covariate"]],     new_covariate = data_list[["new_covariate"]],     important_feature = copula_fit[["important_feature"]],     filtered_gene = data_list[["filtered_gene"]],     parallelization = "pbmcapply"     )  RNGkind("Mersenne-Twister")  # reset

what: Step 4: create SingleCellExperiment object using simulated data
title: Modeling batch effect in population-scale scRNA-seq data
text: simulating data, can create SingleCellExperiment object follows.
code: simu_sce_diff <- SingleCellExperiment(list(counts = newcount_mat_diff),                                   colData = data_list[["new_covariate"]]) names(assays(simu_sce_diff)) <- "counts"  rowData(simu_sce_diff) <- rowData(example_sce)

title: Modeling batch effect in population-scale scRNA-seq data
text: simulated data can visualized using UMAP plot follows.
code: logcounts(example_sce) <- log1p(counts(example_sce)) logcounts(simu_sce) <- log1p(counts(simu_sce)) logcounts(simu_sce_diff) <- log1p(counts(simu_sce_diff))  # For pools set.seed(123) compare_figure <- scDesignPop::plotReducedDimPop(     ref_sce = example_sce,     sce_list = list(simu_sce, simu_sce_diff),      name_vec = c("Reference", "w/o batch effects", "w/ batch effects"),     assay_use = "logcounts",      if_plot = TRUE,      color_by = "pool", point_size = 0.1,     n_pc = 30)  plot(compare_figure$p_umap) # For cell types set.seed(123) compare_figure <- scDesignPop::plotReducedDimPop(     ref_sce = example_sce,     sce_list = list(simu_sce, simu_sce_diff),      name_vec = c("Reference", "w/o batch effects", "w/ batch effects"),     assay_use = "logcounts",      if_plot = TRUE,      color_by = "cell_type", point_size = 0.1,     n_pc = 30)  plot(compare_figure$p_umap)

title: Model cell type proportions for new individuals
text: scDesignPop provide modeling cell type proportions generating new cell type proportions new simulated individuals. default covariates used PCs genotypes, show following tutorial.

title: Model cell type proportions for new individuals
text: , load example_sce data genotype PCs training example_sce data new simulated individuals.

title: Model cell type proportions for new individuals
text: model cell type proportions Multinomial model using genotype PCs covariates. also model total cell number per individuals log-normal distribution. , indiv_colname used specify shared column name individual ids colData(example_sce) genopc_train. use celltype_colname specify column name cell states cell types colData(example_sce). , input othercov_new specify individual ids simulated test data.
code: set.seed(123) simu_cellprop_list <- simuCellProportion(   sce = example_sce,   genoPC = example_genopc_train,   new_genoPC = example_genopc_new,   new_othercov = othercov_new,   PCnum = 30L,   indiv_colname = "indiv",   celltype_colname = "cell_type",   cn_model_family = "lognormal",    # cell number model   cp_model_family = "MN",  # cell proportion model   cp_intercept = TRUE )

title: Model nonlinear dynamic eQTL effects in continuous cell states
text: scDesignPop can also extended simulate nonlinear dynamic eQTL effects. , show scDesignPop can also model nonlinear dynamic eQTL effects continuous cell states mimick real data better. scDesignPop can model continuous cell states via marginal fitting step.

title: Model nonlinear dynamic eQTL effects in continuous cell states
text: , use subset B cells OneK1K cohort example. load example SingleCellExperiment object example_sce 753753 genes 37853785 cells example eQTL genotype dataframe example_eqtlgeno demonstrate main tutorial. two objects contains gene expression SNP genotypes 100100 anonymized individuals eQTL genotype dataframe provides 23322332 putative cell-type-specific eQTLs. nonlinear dynamic eQTL modeling, quadratic pseudotime term slingPseudotime_1_sq added capture nonlinearity.
code: library(scDesignPop) library(SingleCellExperiment) library(SummarizedExperiment) library(scater)  data("example_sce_Bcell") data("example_eqtlgeno_Bcell")  col_Bcell <- colData(example_sce_Bcell) colData(example_sce_Bcell)$slingPseudotime_1_sq <- col_Bcell$slingPseudotime_1^2 head(colData(example_sce_Bcell)) #> DataFrame with 6 rows and 6 columns #>          indiv cell_type      sex       age slingPseudotime_1 #>       <factor>  <factor> <factor> <integer>         <numeric> #> Cell1   SAMP68      bin         1        69          0.288526 #> Cell2   SAMP68      bin         1        69          0.396471 #> Cell3   SAMP68      bin         1        69          0.574161 #> Cell4   SAMP68      bmem        1        69          1.000000 #> Cell5   SAMP68      bin         1        69          0.525864 #> Cell6   SAMP68      bin         1        69          0.516429 #>       slingPseudotime_1_sq #>                  <numeric> #> Cell1             0.083247 #> Cell2             0.157189 #> Cell3             0.329660 #> Cell4             1.000000 #> Cell5             0.276533 #> Cell6             0.266699

title: Model nonlinear dynamic eQTL effects in continuous cell states
text: run scDesignPop, list data required input. done using constructDataPop function. SingleCellExperiment object eqtlgeno dataframe two main inputs needed. eqtlgeno dataframe consists eQTL annotations (must cell state, gene, SNP, chromosome, position columns minimum), genotypes across individuals (columns) every SNP (rows). structure example eqtlgeno dataframe given .
code: data_list <- constructDataPop(     sce = example_sce_Bcell,     eqtlgeno_df = example_eqtlgeno_Bcell,     new_covariate = as.data.frame(colData(example_sce_Bcell)),     copula_variable = "slingPseudotime_1",     slot_name = "counts",     snp_mode = "single",     time_colname = "slingPseudotime_1"     )

title: Model nonlinear dynamic eQTL effects in continuous cell states
text: Next, marginal model specified fit gene using fitMarginalPop function. use Negative Binominal parametric model using "nb".
code: marginal_list <- fitMarginalPop(     data_list = data_list,     mean_formula = "(1|indiv) + slingPseudotime_1 + slingPseudotime_1_sq",     model_family = "nb",     interact_colnames = c("slingPseudotime_1", "slingPseudotime_1_sq"),     parallelization = "parallel",     n_threads = 20L     )

title: Model nonlinear dynamic eQTL effects in continuous cell states
text: third step fit Gaussian copula using fitCopulaPop function.
code: set.seed(123, kind = "L'Ecuyer-CMRG")  copula_fit <- fitCopulaPop(     sce = example_sce_Bcell,     assay_use = "counts",     input_data = data_list[["covariate"]],     marginal_list = marginal_list,     family_use = "nb",     copula = "gaussian",     n_cores = 2L,     parallelization = "parallel"     )  RNGkind("Mersenne-Twister")  # reset

title: Model nonlinear dynamic eQTL effects in continuous cell states
text: fourth step compute mean, sigma, zero probability parameters using extractParaPop function.
code: para_new <- extractParaPop(     sce = example_sce_Bcell,     assay_use = "counts",     marginal_list = marginal_list,     n_cores = 2L,     family_use = "nb",     new_covariate = data_list[["new_covariate"]],     new_eqtl_geno_list = data_list[["eqtl_geno_list"]],     data = data_list[["covariate"]],     parallelization = "parallel"     )

title: Model nonlinear dynamic eQTL effects in continuous cell states
text: fifth step simulate counts using simuNewPop function.

title: Model linear dynamic eQTL effects in continuous cell states
text: scDesignPop can also extended simulate linear dynamic eQTL effects. , show scDesignPop can also model linear dynamic eQTL effects continuous cell states mimick real data better. scDesignPop can model continuous cell states via marginal fitting step.

title: Model linear dynamic eQTL effects in continuous cell states
text: , use subset B cells OneK1K cohort example. load example SingleCellExperiment object example_sce 753753 genes 37853785 cells example eQTL genotype dataframe example_eqtlgeno demonstrate main tutorial. two objects contains gene expression SNP genotypes 100100 anonymized individuals eQTL genotype dataframe provides 23322332 putative cell-type-specific eQTLs.
code: library(scDesignPop) library(SingleCellExperiment) library(SummarizedExperiment) library(scater)  data("example_sce_Bcell") data("example_eqtlgeno_Bcell") head(colData(example_sce_Bcell)) #> DataFrame with 6 rows and 5 columns #>          indiv cell_type      sex       age slingPseudotime_1 #>       <factor>  <factor> <factor> <integer>         <numeric> #> Cell1   SAMP68      bin         1        69          0.288526 #> Cell2   SAMP68      bin         1        69          0.396471 #> Cell3   SAMP68      bin         1        69          0.574161 #> Cell4   SAMP68      bmem        1        69          1.000000 #> Cell5   SAMP68      bin         1        69          0.525864 #> Cell6   SAMP68      bin         1        69          0.516429

title: Model linear dynamic eQTL effects in continuous cell states
text: scDesignPop can also extended eQTL effects. show scDesignPop can model dynamic eQTL effects continuous cell states potential -silico ground-truth. consider linear dynamic eQTL effects simplicity. run scDesignPop, list data required input. done using constructDataPop function. SingleCellExperiment object eqtlgeno dataframe two main inputs needed. eqtlgeno dataframe consists eQTL annotations (must cell state, gene, SNP, chromosome, position columns minimum), genotypes across individuals (columns) every SNP (rows). structure example eqtlgeno dataframe given .
code: data_list <- constructDataPop(     sce = example_sce_Bcell,     eqtlgeno_df = example_eqtlgeno_Bcell,     new_covariate = as.data.frame(colData(example_sce_Bcell)),     copula_variable = "slingPseudotime_1",     slot_name = "counts",     snp_mode = "single",     time_colname = "slingPseudotime_1"     )

title: Model linear dynamic eQTL effects in continuous cell states
text: Next, marginal model specified fit gene using fitMarginalPop function. use Negative Binominal parametric model using "nb".
code: marginal_list <- fitMarginalPop(     data_list = data_list,     mean_formula = "(1|indiv) + slingPseudotime_1",     model_family = "nb",     interact_colnames = c("slingPseudotime_1"),     parallelization = "parallel",     n_threads = 20L     )

title: Model linear dynamic eQTL effects in continuous cell states
text: third step fit Gaussian copula using fitCopulaPop function.
code: set.seed(123, kind = "L'Ecuyer-CMRG")  copula_fit <- fitCopulaPop(     sce = example_sce_Bcell,     assay_use = "counts",     input_data = data_list[["covariate"]],     marginal_list = marginal_list,     family_use = "nb",     copula = "gaussian",     n_cores = 2L,     parallelization = "parallel"     )  RNGkind("Mersenne-Twister")  # reset

title: Model linear dynamic eQTL effects in continuous cell states
text: fourth step compute mean, sigma, zero probability parameters using extractParaPop function.
code: para_new <- extractParaPop(     sce = example_sce_Bcell,     assay_use = "counts",     marginal_list = marginal_list,     n_cores = 2L,     family_use = "nb",     new_covariate = data_list[["new_covariate"]],     new_eqtl_geno_list = data_list[["eqtl_geno_list"]],     data = data_list[["covariate"]],     parallelization = "parallel"     )

title: Model linear dynamic eQTL effects in continuous cell states
text: fifth step simulate counts using simuNewPop function.
code: set.seed(123, kind = "L'Ecuyer-CMRG")  newcount_mat <- simuNewPop(     sce = example_sce_Bcell,     mean_mat = para_new[["mean_mat"]],     sigma_mat = para_new[["sigma_mat"]],     zero_mat = para_new[["zero_mat"]],     quantile_mat = NULL,     copula_list = copula_fit[["copula_list"]],     n_cores = 2L,     family_use = "nb",     nonnegative = TRUE,     input_data = data_list[["covariate"]],     new_covariate = data_list[["new_covariate"]],     important_feature = copula_fit[["important_feature"]],     filtered_gene = data_list[["filtered_gene"]],     parallelization = "parallel"     )  RNGkind("Mersenne-Twister")  # reset

title: Modify eQTL effect for eGenes / non-eGenes
text: scDesignPop enables users modify estimated cell-type-specific eQTL effect sizes generate realistic scRNA-seq data altered ground truths. data allow downstream tasks systematic benchmarking various eQTL mapping methods different ground truths.

title: Modify eQTL effect for eGenes / non-eGenes
text: , use example SingleCellExperiment object example_sce 982982 genes 79987998 cells example eQTL genotype dataframe example_eqtlgeno demonstrate tutorial. two objects contains gene expression SNP genotypes 4040 anonymized individuals eQTL genotype dataframe provides 27922792 putative cell-type-specific eQTLs.
code: library(scDesignPop) library(SingleCellExperiment) library(SummarizedExperiment) library(ggplot2)  data("example_sce") data("example_eqtlgeno")

title: Modify eQTL effect for eGenes / non-eGenes
text: run scDesignPop, list data required input. done using constructDataPop function. SingleCellExperiment object eqtlgeno dataframe two main inputs needed. eqtlgeno dataframe consists eQTL annotations (must cell state, gene, SNP, chromosome, position columns minimum), genotypes across individuals (columns) every SNP (rows). structure example eqtlgeno dataframe given .
code: data_list <- constructDataPop(     sce = example_sce,     eqtlgeno_df = example_eqtlgeno,     new_covariate = as.data.frame(colData(example_sce)),     copula_variable = "cell_type",     slot_name = "counts",     snp_mode = "single"     ) #> slot_name argument is deprecated and will be removed in a future update.Please use assay_use instead. #> Constructing eqtlgeno list...

title: Modify eQTL effect for eGenes / non-eGenes
text: Next, marginal model specified fit gene using fitMarginalPop function. use Negative Binominal mixed model using "nb" option. specify "(1|indiv) + cell_type" mean formula, (1|indiv) specifies random intercept using indiv variable.
code: marginal_list <- fitMarginalPop(     data_list = data_list,     mean_formula = "(1|indiv) + cell_type",     model_family = "nb",     interact_colnames = "cell_type",     parallelization = "parallel",     n_threads = 20L     ) #> n_threads argument is deprecated and will be removed in a future update.Please use n_cores instead.

text: third step fit Gaussian copula using fitCopulaPop function.

title: Parallel computing and reproducibility in scDesignPop
text: scDesignPop currently supports several different backend options parallel computing computationally intensive functions. tutorial split two parts: first, explain differences parallelization options; second, explain parallel computing offered random number generation (RNG) occurs scDesignPop’s simulation workflow.

title: Parallel computing and reproducibility in scDesignPop
text: Currently, scDesignPop leverages parallel, pbmcapply, BiocParallel, future.apply R packages parallel computing. backends differ operating system support, reproducibility guarantees, usability.

title: Parallel computing and reproducibility in scDesignPop
text: parallel package offers parallelization using apply-like functions mclapply() mcmapply. uses forking parallel processing, availabe Linux macOS (M-series processors) systems, Windows unless mc.cores = 1. default, using set.seed() guarantee reproducibility parallel execution. can seen example , two sets 1010 random numbers generated standard Gaussian distribution produce different results despite using seed. ensure reproducibility, RNG method must set "L'Ecuyer-CMRG", supports parallel-safe random number streams. Since modifies global RNG settings, good practice afterwards reset default, "Mersenne-Twister" R.

title: Parallel computing and reproducibility in scDesignPop
text: pbmcapply package extends parallel adding progress bar pbmclapply() pbmcmapply() functions. provides estimated completion time, can useful long-running simulations. Like parallel package, relies forking, thus supports parallel execution Linux macOS (M-series processors) mc.cores > 1. Another key difference pbmcapply currently generate reproducible code RNG present within apply code. demonstrated example using "L'Ecuyer-CMRG" RNG method. behavior noted issue various discussions, noted .

title: Parallel computing and reproducibility in scDesignPop
text: BiocParallel package provides parallel computing across different operating system platforms. practice, two relevant backends used scDesignPop MulticoreParam SnowParam. MulticoreParam uses forking therefore available Linux macOS, whereas SnowParam uses separate R worker processes available Windows, Linux, macOS. makes BiocParallel convenient cross-operating system option. BiocParallel built-support reproducible random number generation via RNGseed argument instead using set.seed(). Given seed, identical results returned across repeated runs, invariant backend number workers used. toy example illustrates using SnowParam MulticoreParam, well using different number workers. Lastly, progress bar can enabled using progressbar = TRUE option.

title: Parallel computing and reproducibility in scDesignPop
text: future.apply package offers parallel computing using future framework. two parallel computing options, multisession multicore, can specified using plan(). multisession works across Windows, Linux, macOS launching separate R sessions, whereas multicore uses forking therefore limited Linux, macOS systems. future.apply support reproducible RNG, warning produced RNG detected using functions default options. generate reproducible RNG, future.seed argument must specified. option ensures results returned across repeated runs given initial seed, regardless future backend used number workers. Since plan() changes global environment R, ’s usually good idea reset back default. following example generates reproducible random numbers parallel: One caveat reproducibility future.apply can introduce overhead, seeds generated advance iterations. may increase computation time memory use.
code: library(future) library(future.apply)  unlist(future_lapply(1:10, function(i) rnorm(1))) #> Warning: UNRELIABLE VALUE: One of the 'future.apply' iterations #> ('future_lapply-1') unexpectedly generated random numbers without declaring so. #> There is a risk that those random numbers are not statistically sound and the #> overall results might be invalid. To fix this, specify 'future.seed=TRUE'. This #> ensures that proper, parallel-safe random numbers are produced via the #> L'Ecuyer-CMRG method. To disable this check, use 'future.seed = NULL', or set #> option 'future.rng.onMisuse' to "ignore". #>  [1] -0.1207144 -0.7934806  0.2565556  0.4045028  1.9082073  1.6040098 #>  [7] -1.7511067 -0.5945040  0.4369179  0.5360741 plan(multisession, workers = 5)  unlist(future_lapply(1:10, function(i) rnorm(1), future.seed = 123)) #>  [1] -1.01425025 -0.03074715  0.08281673 -0.96674384  0.30474400  0.37906458 #>  [7]  0.01244892  0.83755002  0.05058956  0.10957834 unlist(future_lapply(1:10, function(i) rnorm(1), future.seed = 123)) #>  [1] -1.01425025 -0.03074715  0.08281673 -0.96674384  0.30474400  0.37906458 #>  [7]  0.01244892  0.83755002  0.05058956  0.10957834  plan("default")

title: Parallel computing and reproducibility in scDesignPop
text: Parallel computing currently supported scDesignPop’s fitMarginalPop(), fitCopulaPop(), extractParaPop(), simuNewPop(), powerAnalysis() functions. computationally intensive parts scDesignPop’s workflow. four different parallel backend options supported follows: parallel pbmcparallel, parallel computing supported scDesignPop using parallelization = "parallel" parallelization = "pbmcapply". BiocParallel, backend option enabled scDesignPop using parallelization = "biocparallel". Note requires initializing either class object using BPPARAM = MulticoreParam() BPPARAM = SnowParam(). future.apply, backend option enabled scDesignPop using parallelization = "future.apply". Currently, multisession option supported offers best cross-operating system compatibility. number CPU cores used controlled using n_cores argument backend parallelization options. example, show parallel BiocParallel can used impact data reproducibility typical scDesignPop workflow.

title: Parallel computing and reproducibility in scDesignPop
text: use subset 200200 genes example_sce data marginal fitting. , specify negative binomial mixed model using model_family = "nb", individuals random intercept cell type fixed effect (1|indiv) + cell_type. specify cell-type-specific eQTLs, use single-SNP mode via snp_mode = "single" genotype-cell-type interactions via interact_colnames = "cell_type". scDesignPop’s marginal fitting process largely deterministic (.e., RNG present), using pbmcapply still generates reproducible marginal fittings. , confirm fitted gene 22 repeated marginal fittings . , use .equal() compare fitted gene two marginal list objects may negligible differences (<2.220446×10−16\lt 2.220446 \times 10^{-16}) numerical optimization process.
code: gene_subset <- rownames(example_sce)[1:200]  data_list <- constructDataPop(     sce = example_sce,     eqtlgeno_df = example_eqtlgeno,     new_covariate = as.data.frame(colData(example_sce)),     copula_variable = "cell_type",     slot_name = "counts",     snp_mode = "single",     overlap_features = gene_subset     ) #> slot_name argument is deprecated and will be removed in a future update.Please use assay_use instead. #> Constructing eqtlgeno list...  marginal_list <- fitMarginalPop(     data_list = data_list,     mean_formula = "(1|indiv) + cell_type",     model_family = "nb",     interact_colnames = "cell_type",     parallelization = "pbmcapply",     n_cores = 10L )  marginal_list2 <- fitMarginalPop(     data_list = data_list,     mean_formula = "(1|indiv) + cell_type",     model_family = "nb",     interact_colnames = "cell_type",     parallelization = "pbmcapply",     n_cores = 10L )  # reproducibility check all(unlist(lapply(1:length(marginal_list), function(x) {      all.equal(marginal_list[[x]]$fit, marginal_list2[[x]]$fit)     }))) #> Warning in all(unlist(lapply(1:length(marginal_list), function(x) {: coercing #> argument of type 'character' to logical #> [1] NA

title: Power analysis based on user specified eQTL effect sizes
text: Given fitted marginal models, scDesignPop can perform simulation-based power analysis selected gene–SNP pair using runPowerAnalysis. function evaluates often eQTL detected user-specified effect sizes, study designs, eQTL mapping models. example, focus gene ENSG00000163221 SNP 1:153337943, assess power classical monocytes (monoc). main arguments define target cell type, assumed eQTL effect size scenarios, sample-size settings, methods (eQTL mapping models) used testing. Several inputs particularly important: celltype_specific_ES_list specifies effect size scenarios evaluate. list element corresponds one scenario can assign effect sizes one cell types. nindivs ncells specify number individuals cells per individual, respectively, determining study design. methods specifies eQTL analysis methods compare. alpha, snp_number, gene_number determine significance threshold multiple testing correction. power_nsim controls number simulation replicates used estimate power, CI_nsim CI_conf control bootstrap confidence intervals. Additional arguments nPool, nIndivPerPool, nCellPerPool can used evaluate pooled sequencing designs relevant.
code: set.seed(123)  power_data <- runPowerAnalysis(     marginal_list = marginal_list_sel,     marginal_model = "nb",     geneid = "ENSG00000163221",     snpid = "1:153337943",     celltype_colname = "cell_type",     celltype_vector = c("monoc"),     celltype_specific_ES_list = list(         c("monoc" = 0.05),         c("monoc" = 0.1),         c("monoc" = 0.2)     ),     indiv_colname = "indiv",     methods = c("poisson", "pseudoBulkLinear"),     nindivs = c(50, 200),     ncells = c(10, 50),     alpha = 0.05,     power_nsim = 1000,     snp_number = 10,     gene_number = 800,     CI_nsim = 1000,     CI_conf = 0.05,     n_cores = 25 ) #> [1] 1.920313 #> [1] 0.05 #> [1] 1.920313 #> [1] 0.1 #> [1] 1.920313 #> [1] 0.2 #> [1] 1.920313 #> [1] 0.05 #> [1] 1.920313 #> [1] 0.1 #> [1] 1.920313 #> [1] 0.2

title: Power analysis based on user specified eQTL effect sizes
text: results can visualized using visualizePowerCurve. allows us examine power varies function effect size, sample size, analysis method. plot , visualize power function specified effect size, stratified number individuals cells. expect power increase larger effect sizes, individuals, cells per individual.  Alternatively, can swap faceting variables view results different perspective.
code: visualizePowerCurve(     power_result = power_data,     celltype_vector = c("monoc"),     x_axis = "specifiedES",     x_facet = "nindiv",     y_facet = "ncell",     col_group = "method",     geneid = "ENSG00000163221",     snpid = "1:153337943" ) visualizePowerCurve(     power_result = power_data,     celltype_vector = c("monoc"),     x_axis = "specifiedES",     x_facet = "ncell",     y_facet = "nindiv",     col_group = "method",     geneid = "ENSG00000163221",     snpid = "1:153337943" )

title: Power analysis for selected genes
text: addition user-specified effect size scenarios, scDesignPop also supports data-driven power analysis based fitted marginal models. setting, effect sizes inferred reference data, power evaluated observed settings. tutorial demonstrates two usage modes: Fit--analyze workflow: fit marginal models raw data perform power analysis Reuse--analyze workflow: directly perform power analysis using previously fitted marginal models Basically, marginal models already available (e.g., simulation previous analysis), users can skip data preparation model fitting steps.

title: Power analysis for selected genes
text: begin loading example dataset selecting small subset genes demonstration. reduces computational cost preserving workflow structure. construct input data required modeling, including expression, covariates, genotype information.
code: library(scDesignPop) library(SingleCellExperiment)  data("example_sce") data("example_eqtlgeno")  example_sce_sel <- example_sce[c("ENSG00000163221","ENSG00000135218"),]  example_eqtlgeno_sel <- example_eqtlgeno[     which(example_eqtlgeno$gene_id %in% c("ENSG00000163221","ENSG00000135218")), ] data_list_sel <- constructDataPop(     sce = example_sce_sel,     eqtlgeno_df = example_eqtlgeno_sel,     new_covariate = as.data.frame(colData(example_sce_sel)),     copula_variable = "cell_type",     slot_name = "counts",     snp_mode = "single" )

title: Power analysis for selected genes
text: next fit marginal models using fitMarginalPop. models describe relationship gene expression, covariates, genotype, provide basis simulation-based power analysis. inspect fitted model specific gene, can examine model summary:

title: Power analysis for selected genes
text: now perform power analysis using runPowerAnalysis. contrast previous tutorial, manually specify effect sizes. Instead, function uses effect sizes estimated marginal models, making data-driven evaluation power. example, assess power gene ENSG00000163221 SNP 1:153337943 across two cell types (bmem monoc). main inputs define: geneid, snpid: gene–SNP pair evaluate celltype_vector: cell types test nindivs: number individuals ncells: number cells per individual methods: eQTL mapping models compare alpha: unadjusted significance threshold snp_number, gene_number: multiple testing correction effect sizes taken fitted models, analysis reflects power eQTL effects observed reference dataset. resulting power_data summarizes estimated power across combinations study design eQTL mapping models.
code: set.seed(123)  power_data <- runPowerAnalysis(     marginal_list = marginal_list_sel,     marginal_model = "nb",     geneid = "ENSG00000163221",     snpid = "1:153337943",     celltype_colname = "cell_type",     celltype_vector = c("bmem", "monoc"),     indiv_colname = "indiv",     methods = c("poisson", "pseudoBulkLinear"),     nindivs = c(50, 200),     ncells = c(10, 50),     alpha = 0.05,     power_nsim = 1000,     snp_number = 10,     gene_number = 800,     CI_nsim = 1000,     CI_conf = 0.05,     n_cores = 25 ) #> [1] -5.036509 #> [1] 0.2514451 #> [1] 1.920313 #> [1] -0.2320467 #> [1] -5.036509 #> [1] 0.2514451 #> [1] 1.920313 #> [1] -0.2320467

title: Power analysis for selected genes
text: results can visualized using visualizePowerCurve. allows us examine power varies function effect size, sample size, analysis method. plot , visualize power function specified effect size, stratified number individuals cells. expect power increase larger effect sizes, individuals, cells per individual.  Alternatively, can swap axes view relationship different perspective:
code: visualizePowerCurve(     power_result = power_data,     celltype_vector = c("bmem", "monoc"),     x_axis = "nindiv",     y_facet = "ncell",     col_group = "method",     geneid = "ENSG00000163221",     snpid = "1:153337943" ) visualizePowerCurve(     power_result = power_data,     celltype_vector = c("bmem", "monoc"),     x_axis = "ncell",     y_facet = "nindiv",     col_group = "method",     geneid = "ENSG00000163221",     snpid = "1:153337943" )

title: Power analysis for selected genes
text: better summarize optimal study design choices, can visualize power across combinations individuals cells using heatmap. representation makes easier identify regions high power compare performance across cell types methods.
code: visualizePowerHeatmap(     power_result = power_data,     nindiv_col   = "nindiv",     ncell_col    = "ncell",     x_facet      = "celltype",     y_facet      = "method",     power_col    = "power",     fill_label   = "Power",     fill_limits  = c(0, 1),     facet_scales = "fixed",     base_size    = 12 )

title: Modeling trans-eQTL effect using public data
text: scDesignPop supports user-defined eQTL pairs, making straightforward extend framework trans-eQTL studies known ground truth.

title: Modeling trans-eQTL effect using public data
text: , use example trans-eQTL genotype dataframe example_eqtlgeno_trans demonstrate scDesignPop can also model trans-eQTL effects. Based trans-eQTL list eQTLGen phase 1 (https://www.eqtlgen.org/trans-eqtls.html), constructed example genotype dataframe permuted genotypes show trans-eQTL effects can preserved scDesignPop simulation. download link full trans-eQTL list https://download.gcc.rug.nl/downloads/eqtlgen/trans-eqtl/2018-09-04-trans-eQTLsFDR0.05-CohortInfoRemoved-BonferroniAdded.txt.gz.
code: library(scDesignPop) library(SingleCellExperiment) library(SummarizedExperiment) library(ggplot2) library(data.table)  data("example_sce") data("example_eqtlgeno_trans")  example_sce #> class: SingleCellExperiment  #> dim: 982 7998  #> metadata(0): #> assays(1): counts #> rownames(982): ENSG00000023902 ENSG00000028137 ... ENSG00000254709 #>   ENSG00000273272 #> rowData names(0): #> colnames(7998): Cell1 Cell2 ... Cell7997 Cell7998 #> colData names(5): indiv pool cell_type sex age #> reducedDimNames(0): #> mainExpName: NULL #> altExpNames(0): head(example_eqtlgeno_trans[,1:8]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 8 #>   cell_type gene_id         snp_id        CHR       POS SAMP1 SAMP2 SAMP3 #>   <chr>     <chr>           <chr>       <int>     <int> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 bulk      ENSG00000159674 10:94450233    10  94450233     2     2     1 #> 2 bulk      ENSG00000110077 2:182323766     2 182323766     2     1     1 #> 3 bulk      ENSG00000117281 10:94450233    10  94450233     2     2     1 #> 4 bulk      ENSG00000149557 10:94450233    10  94450233     2     2     1 #> 5 bulk      ENSG00000006075 10:94450233    10  94450233     2     2     1 #> 6 bulk      ENSG00000163453 10:94450233    10  94450233     2     2     1

title: Modeling trans-eQTL effect using public data
text: run scDesignPop, list data required input. done using constructDataPop function. SingleCellExperiment object eqtlgeno dataframe two main inputs needed. eqtlgeno dataframe consists eQTL annotations (must cell state, gene, SNP, chromosome, position columns minimum), genotypes across individuals (columns) every SNP (rows). , use example_sce filter overlapped genes example_eqtlgeno_trans object created
code: overlap_genes <- intersect(rownames(example_sce), example_eqtlgeno_trans$gene_id) example_eqtlgeno_trans <- example_eqtlgeno_trans[which(example_eqtlgeno_trans$gene_id %in% overlap_genes), ] example_sce <- example_sce[which(rownames(example_sce) %in% overlap_genes), ] data_list <- constructDataPop(     sce = example_sce,     eqtlgeno_df = example_eqtlgeno_trans,     new_covariate = as.data.frame(colData(example_sce)),     copula_variable = "cell_type",     slot_name = "counts",     snp_mode = "single"     )

title: Modeling trans-eQTL effect using public data
text: Next, marginal model specified fit gene using fitMarginalPop function. use Negative Binominal parametric model using "nb".
code: marginal_list <- fitMarginalPop(     data_list = data_list,     mean_formula = "(1|indiv) + cell_type",     model_family = "nb",     interact_colnames = "cell_type",     parallelization = "parallel",     n_threads = 20L     )

title: Modeling trans-eQTL effect using public data
text: third step fit Gaussian copula using fitCopulaPop function.
code: set.seed(123, kind = "L'Ecuyer-CMRG")  copula_fit <- fitCopulaPop(     sce = example_sce,     assay_use = "counts",     input_data = data_list[["covariate"]],     marginal_list = marginal_list,     family_use = "nb",     copula = "gaussian",     n_cores = 2L,     parallelization = "parallel"     )  RNGkind("Mersenne-Twister")  # reset

title: Modeling trans-eQTL effect using public data
text: fourth step compute mean, sigma, zero probability parameters using extractParaPop function.
code: para_new <- extractParaPop(     sce = example_sce,     assay_use = "counts",     marginal_list = marginal_list,     n_cores = 2L,     family_use = "nb",     new_covariate = data_list[["new_covariate"]],     new_eqtl_geno_list = data_list[["eqtl_geno_list"]],     data = data_list[["covariate"]],     parallelization = "parallel"     )

title: Modeling trans-eQTL effect using public data
text: fifth step simulate counts using simuNewPop function.
code: set.seed(123, kind = "L'Ecuyer-CMRG")  newcount_mat <- simuNewPop(     sce = example_sce,     mean_mat = para_new[["mean_mat"]],     sigma_mat = para_new[["sigma_mat"]],     zero_mat = para_new[["zero_mat"]],     copula_list = copula_fit[["copula_list"]],     n_cores = 2L,     family_use = "nb",     input_data = data_list[["covariate"]],     new_covariate = data_list[["new_covariate"]],     important_feature = copula_fit[["important_feature"]],     filtered_gene = data_list[["filtered_gene"]],     parallelization = "parallel"     )  RNGkind("Mersenne-Twister")  # reset

title: Modeling trans-eQTL effect using public data

what: Create and visualize pseudobulk expression data
title: Modeling trans-eQTL effect using public data
text: Next, create pseudobulk data normalizing SCE object data using log1p aggregate using mean. specify gene trans-eQTL loci well using createPbulkExprGeno function. Finally, visualize pseudobulk expression gene loci.
code: res_list <- createPbulkExprGeno(sce_list = list("Reference" = example_sce,                                                 "Simulated" = simu_sce),                                 eqtlgeno = example_eqtlgeno_trans,                                 feature_sel = "ENSG00000006075",                                 celltype_sel = "cd4nc",                                 eqtl_snp = "10:94450233",                                 normalize_type = "log1p",                                 aggregate_type = "mean",                                 slot_name = "logcounts",                                 overwrite = TRUE,                                 if_plot = TRUE) res_list[["p_pbulk"]]

title: scDesignPop Quickstart
text: Population-scale single-cell RNA-sequencing (scRNA-seq) provides unprecedented opportunity study genetic variation shapes gene expression cellular resolution. However, generating datasets remains costly, methodological choices eQTL analysis still lacks concensus, sharing individual-level data raises privacy concerns. scDesignPop generative framework designed address challenges simulating realistic population-scale single-cell datasets genetic effects. learning reference dataset, enables users generate synthetic data preserves key biological statistical properties, providing flexibility study design, benchmarking, privacy-aware data sharing.

title: scDesignPop Quickstart
text: tutorial, demonstrate minimal workflow using scDesignPop. Starting reference dataset consisting gene expression genotype information, construct model inputs, fit gene-level marginal joint models, finally generate simulated datasets.

title: scDesignPop Quickstart
text: begin loading required packages example datasets provided scDesignPop.
code: library(scDesignPop) library(SingleCellExperiment) library(SummarizedExperiment) library(ggplot2) theme_set(theme_bw())  data("example_sce") data("example_eqtlgeno")

title: scDesignPop Quickstart
text: begin preparing reference data downstream modeling simulation using constructDataPop. function extracts expression matrix cell-level covariates SingleCellExperiment object, processes eQTL/genotype dataframe gene-specific genotype inputs, filters features SNPs needed. resulting objects provide standardized interface remaining steps scDesignPop workflow. two main inputs SingleCellExperiment object (sce) eQTL/genotype dataframe (eqtlgeno_df). SingleCellExperiment object stores expression matrix together cell-level metadata, whereas eQTL/genotype dataframe contains eQTL annotations SNP genotypes across individuals. minimum, eQTL/genotype dataframe include columns specifying gene, SNP, chromosome, genomic position, cell state cell type, followed genotype columns individual. example, use raw count matrix stored "counts" assay example_sce, provide new_covariate = .data.frame(colData(example_sce)) simulation performed using cell-level covariate structure reference data. also set copula_variable = "cell_type", means copula model later fitted separately within cell type. Several arguments used tell constructDataPop variables named input data: slot_name = "counts" specifies assay SingleCellExperiment object used expression matrix. cases, "counts" UMI count data. celltype_colname = "cell_type" specifies column eqtlgeno_df cell metadata sce contains cell type labels. feature_colname = "gene_id" specifies gene identifier column eqtlgeno_df. snp_colname = "snp_id" specifies SNP identifier column eqtlgeno_df. chrom_colname = "CHR" loc_colname = "POS" specify chromosome genomic position columns SNP annotation. indiv_colname = "indiv" specifies individual identifier column cell metadata sce. column-name arguments changed match user’s data. example, gene column eQTL dataframe called gene, feature_colname = "gene" used instead. arguments snp_mode copula_variable determine two important modeling choices: snp_mode = "single" indicates gene modeled using single SNP. appropriate eQTL input already defines one SNP per gene, single lead SNP used gene. snp_mode = "multi" allows multiple SNPs per gene. case, SNPs pruned based genotype correlation, prune_thres controlling correlation threshold used pruning. copula_variable = "cell_type" means copula model fitted separately cell type. copula_variable = NULL, one copula fitted across cells. continuous cell-state information pseudotime available, users can instead set copula_variable = time_colname, time_column specifies column eqtlgeno_df cell metadata sce contains continuous cell state/pseudotime values, case cells grouped time quantiles copula fitting. resulting data_list contains core inputs used throughout pipeline, including expression matrix, cell covariates, filtered feature sets, gene-specific genotype tables used model fitting simulation. , use example data explicitly map required variable column name.
code: data_list <- constructDataPop(     sce = example_sce,     eqtlgeno_df = example_eqtlgeno,     new_covariate = as.data.frame(colData(example_sce)),     copula_variable = "cell_type",     slot_name = "counts",     snp_mode = "single",     celltype_colname = "cell_type",     feature_colname = "gene_id",     snp_colname = "snp_id",     loc_colname = "POS",     chrom_colname = "CHR",     indiv_colname = "indiv" )

title: scDesignPop Quickstart
text: next fit marginal model gene using fitMarginalPop. scDesignPop, marginal model describes distribution expression one gene time function cell-level covariates, individual-level covariates, genetic effects. fitted gene-level models form foundation generative framework, determine mean structure later used simulation. key argument step mean_formula, specifies baseline model gene expression without explicitly including SNP terms. Users provide covariates random effects want include gene expression model, scDesignPop automatically adds SNP genotype effect, together requested first-order SNP interaction terms. design allows genotype effects different SNPs incorporated consistently across genes automatically. example , use includes: - random intercept indiv, account correlation among cells individual - fixed effect cell_type, capture differences baseline expression across cell types also set SNP effects allowed vary across cell types first-order SNP--cell-type interactions. useful goal model cell-type-specific genetic effects. Several arguments control main modeling choices: model_family = "nb" specifies parametric family used model fitting. count-based scRNA-seq data, negative binomial family often natural choice accommodates overdispersion relative Poisson model. mean_formula specifies non-genetic part mean model, including fixed effects random effects. Users include covariates want adjust , cell type, batch, sex, age, biological technical factors. interact_colnames specifies covariates first-order interactions SNP genotype. example, setting interact_colnames = "cell_type" allows genetic effects differ across cell types. interaction needed, can left NULL. parallelization n_cores control gene-wise model fitting parallelized. Since one model fit per gene, step can computationally expensive large datasets, makes parallelization necessary. practice, users tailor mean_formula structure study. example: - data include repeated cells donor, random intercept (1|indiv) often appropriate - batch effects present, batch term can added formula - goal model cell-type-specific eQTL effects, cell_type included formula also specified interact_colnames output marginal_list stores fitted model gene, together SNP covariates used fit.
code: mean_formula = "(1|indiv) + cell_type" interact_colnames = "cell_type" marginal_list <- fitMarginalPop(     data_list = data_list,     mean_formula = "(1|indiv) + cell_type",     model_family = "nb",     interact_colnames = "cell_type",     parallelization = "parallel",     n_threads = 20L,     loc_colname = "POS",     snp_colname = "snp_id",     celltype_colname = "cell_type",     indiv_colname = "indiv" )

title: scDesignPop Quickstart
text: marginal models fitted previous step describe gene individually, capture dependence genes. preserve realistic co-expression structure simulated data, scDesignPop next fits copula model using fitCopulaPop. step links fitted marginal distributions across genes estimates joint dependence structure. scDesignPop, copulas fitted within predefined correlation groups, specified corr_group variable input_data. groups determined constructDataPop copula_variable argument. example, set copula_variable = "cell_type" Step 1, copula fitted separately within cell type. allows simulated data preserve cell-type-specific gene–gene correlation patterns. Several arguments control copula fitted: sce assay_use specify reference expression data used estimate dependence. use count matrix stored "counts" assay. input_data provides cell-level covariates must contain corr_group variable defines cells grouped copula fitting. marginal_list contains fitted gene-level models Step 2 provides marginal distributions needed construct joint model. family_use = "nb" match family used marginal fitting step. copula = "gaussian" specifies Gaussian copula used model dependence correlation matrix. parallelization n_cores control parallelization options number cores used fitting. output step includes fitted copula correlation group, together summary measures overall AIC BIC. copulas used simulation step generate correlated expression values across genes.
code: set.seed(123, kind = "L'Ecuyer-CMRG")  copula_fit <- fitCopulaPop(     sce = example_sce,     assay_use = "counts",     input_data = data_list[["covariate"]],     marginal_list = marginal_list,     family_use = "nb",     copula = "gaussian",     n_cores = 2L,     parallelization = "parallel" )  RNGkind("Mersenne-Twister")

title: scDesignPop Quickstart
text: fitting marginal models, convert explicit parameter matrices simulation using extractParaPop. Given target covariate design genotype configuration, function evaluates fitted models returns cell--gene parameters required data generation. Conceptually, step applies fitted marginal models cells want simulate. words, model coefficients learned reference data combined new_covariate new_eqtl_geno_list inputs determine expected expression parameters simulated cell gene. Several arguments particularly important step: new_covariate cell--covariate data frame defines cells simulated. contain covariates used model fitting, including corr_group variable needed downstream copula-based simulation. new_eqtl_geno_list provides genotype inputs simulated cells new individuals, organized gene. users want simulate individuals reference data, can use genotype list returned constructDataPop. want simulate new individuals, instead provide genotype data corresponding new samples. family_use match marginal model family used fitMarginalPop. assay_use specifies assay reference SingleCellExperiment object used basis parameter extraction. parallelization n_cores control parallelization option used extract parameters fitted models across genes. output list parameter matrices, cells rows genes columns: mean_mat: conditional mean cell-gene pair sigma_mat: gene-specific dispersion parameter cell-gene pair zero_mat: zero-inflation probability cell-gene pair zero-inflated family used example, use covariate structure genotype inputs reference data, extracted parameters correspond simulation original design.
code: para_new <- extractParaPop(     sce = example_sce,     assay_use = "counts",     marginal_list = marginal_list,     n_cores = 2L,     family_use = "nb",     indiv_colname = "indiv",     new_covariate = data_list[["new_covariate"]],     new_eqtl_geno_list = data_list[["eqtl_geno_list"]],     data = data_list[["covariate"]],     parallelization = "parallel" )

title: scDesignPop Quickstart
text: Finally, generate synthetic gene expression data using simuNewPop. step combines parameter matrices Step 4 copula model Step 3 produce new count matrix preserves gene-specific expression gene–gene dependencies. Conceptually, simulation proceeds two stages. First, copula model used generate correlated latent variables across genes, capturing dependence structure learned reference data. latent variables transformed using marginal distributions defined mean_mat, sigma_mat, zero_mat, resulting simulated expression counts cell gene. main inputs function : mean_mat, sigma_mat, zero_mat, define marginal distribution gene cell copula_list, encodes gene–gene dependence structure new_covariate, specifies covariate design simulated dataset Several arguments control important aspects simulation: family_use = "nb" match marginal model family used previous steps. copula_list provides fitted copula models important_feature determines genes included modeling dependence; default, genes used. filtered_gene contains genes excluded model fitting due potential convergence issues ensures consistent handling simulation. parallelization n_cores control parallelization option used. output gene--cell matrix simulated counts.
code: set.seed(123, kind = "L'Ecuyer-CMRG")  newcount_mat <- simuNewPop(     sce = example_sce,     mean_mat = para_new[["mean_mat"]],     sigma_mat = para_new[["sigma_mat"]],     zero_mat = para_new[["zero_mat"]],     copula_list = copula_fit[["copula_list"]],     n_cores = 2L,     family_use = "nb",     input_data = data_list[["covariate"]],     new_covariate = data_list[["new_covariate"]],     important_feature = copula_fit[["important_feature"]],     filtered_gene = data_list[["filtered_gene"]],     parallelization = "parallel" )  RNGkind("Mersenne-Twister")

title: scDesignPop Quickstart
text: simulated count matrix can transformed SingleCellExperiment object downstream analysis. allows simulated data handled using standard single-cell workflows, consistent reference dataset. step, assign simulated counts "counts" assay, attach corresponding cell-level covariates, reuse gene-level metadata original dataset. ensures simulated data retains feature annotations can analyzed comparable way. counts: simulated gene expression matrix colData: cell-level covariates used simulation rowData: gene-level metadata reference data resulting object can directly used downstream analysis tools normalization, dimensionality reduction, clustering, visualization.
code: simu_sce <- SingleCellExperiment(     list(counts = newcount_mat),     colData = data_list[["new_covariate"]] )  names(assays(simu_sce)) <- "counts" rowData(simu_sce) <- rowData(example_sce)

title: scDesignPop Quickstart
text: next compare reference simulated data using two complementary approaches: low-dimensional UMAP embedding assess global structure, pseudobulk expression examine genotype–expression relationships (eQTL effects).

title: scDesignPop Quickstart
text: useful first comparison examine global structure data shared UMAP space. , apply log1p transformation count matrix visualize datasets using plotReducedDimPop, cells colored cell type. expect simulated data recapitulate major cell-type structure separation observed reference data.
code: logcounts(example_sce) <- log1p(counts(example_sce)) logcounts(simu_sce) <- log1p(counts(simu_sce))  set.seed(123) compare_figure <- plotReducedDimPop(     ref_sce = example_sce,     sce_list = list(simu_sce),     name_vec = c("Reference", "scDesignPop"),     assay_use = "logcounts",     if_plot = TRUE,     color_by = "cell_type",     point_size = 1,     n_pc = 30 ) plot(compare_figure$p_umap)

title: scDesignPop Quickstart
text: assess whether eQTL effects preserved, summarize expression individual level using pseudobulk profiles. normalize expression using log1p, aggregate within individual cell type using mean, visualize example gene–SNP pair. expect similar genotype–expression trends reference simulated data.
code: res_list <- createPbulkExprGeno(     sce_list = list(         "Reference" = example_sce,         "Simulated" = simu_sce     ),     eqtlgeno = example_eqtlgeno,     feature_sel = "ENSG00000169385",     celltype_sel = "monoc",     eqtl_snp = "14:21359808",     normalize_type = "log1p",     aggregate_type = "mean",     slot_name = "logcounts",     overwrite = TRUE,     if_plot = TRUE ) res_list[["p_pbulk"]]

Starting reference dataset, : constructed modeling inputs expression genotype data fit gene-level marginal models capture covariate genetic effects modeled gene–gene dependencies using copula extracted simulation parameters target covariate design generated synthetic gene expression data compared simulated reference data using visualization pseudobulk summaries Together, steps illustrate scDesignPop learns generative model real data uses produce realistic synthetic datasets.

title: scDesignPop Quickstart
text: scDesignPop framework can used several downstream applications: Benchmarking eQTL mapping methods Simulated data known ground truth can used evaluate compare different eQTL analysis pipelines. Power analysis study design Users can vary number individuals, cells, sequencing depth assess statistical power different experimental designs. Privacy-preserving data generation Synthetic datasets can generated simulated genotypes reduce risk re-identification. Modeling dynamic context-specific effects incorporating continuous cell states (e.g., pseudotime), scDesignPop can simulate dynamic eQTL effects across cell states. Similarly, incorporating context (e.g. disease status) interaction genotype effect, scDesignPop can also simulate context-specific eQTL effects. recommend exploring following tutorials advanced usage: Modify eQTL effect eGenes / non-eGenes Power analysis selected genes Model nonlinear dynamic eQTL effects continuous cell states Model trans-eQTL effect using public data tutorials build workflow presented demonstrate tailor scDesignPop specific analysis goals.

title: Modify marginal models — modifyMarginalModels
text: marginal_list list marginal model objects. eqtlgeno_list list eqtl genotypes. features scalar vector features (ie. genes) apply modifications. celltype string specify cell type make modification. neg_ctrl logical value whether set negative control eQTL (ie. non-eGene).  option sets conditional means identical across genotypes (0, 1, 2).  neg_ctrl = TRUE, mean_log2fc option still applied set, eqtl_log2fc overidden impact.  Default FALSE. mean_log2fc numeric scalar vector log2 fold-change parameter increase decrease conditional mean genotype 1 \(\mu_{1}\) cell type. Default mean_log2fc = 0 (parameters modified uses estimated parameters fitted marginal model). eqtl_log2fc numeric scalar vector log2 fold-change parameter increase decrease slope eQTL effect celltype. eQTL slope defined difference conditional mean genotype 1 genotype 0 (\(\mu_{1}\) - \(\mu_{0}\)). Default eqtl_log2fc = mean_log2fc (eQTL slope scaled conditional mean log2 fold-change). eqtl_reverse logical value determine whether eQTL slope trends reverse direction (TRUE) (FALSE). Default FALSE. mean_baseline numeric scalar vector specify minimum conditional mean genotype 1 \(\mu_{1}\).  mean_baseline_only = FALSE, conditional mean maximum fitted (estimated marginal model) mean_baseline value.  Otherwise, conditional mean set mean_baseline value.  Default value NULL. eqtl_baseline numeric scalar vector specify minimum eQTL slope genotype 1 0 (\(\mu_{1}\) - \(\mu_{0}\)).  eqtl_baseline_only = FALSE, eQTL slope maximum slope fitted (estimated marginal model) eqtl_baseline value.  Otherwise, eQTL slope set eqtl_baseline value.  Default value NULL. mean_baseline_only logical scalar vector force conditional mean (linear prediction) genotype 1 \(\mu_{1}\). Default FALSE. eqtl_baseline_only logical scalar vector force eQTL slope genotype 1 0 (\(\mu_{1}\) - \(\mu_{0}\)). Default FALSE. disp_scaling string logical value specify dispersion-mean scaling certain parametric models. Current options either "linear", "quadratic", NULL. FALSE, dispersion parameter changed. default FALSE. (NOTE: currently applicable negative binomial model.) celltype_colname string cell type variable name. snp_colname string SNP id variable name. verbose logical value whether output messages related modified parameters. Default TRUE. debug logical whether output mod_list list addition marginal_list. ... Additional options used. Currently supported: mod_scale string specify scale modified response .  two options 'response' 'link'. default 'response'.
